name: Nahuatl
notes: STRUCTURAL ADSTRATE STUB (not a modelled G2P target). Nahuatl (Aztecan, Uto-Aztecan). The principal indigenous adstrate of central/highland Mexican Spanish; Nawat/Pipil in El Salvador. It exists here only so that the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the Latin American Spanish varieties that reference it as an indigenous adstrate have a node to resolve to; the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder and NO phonology is claimed or modelled (afa.json / ber-x-kabyle-atlas.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Campbell, Lyle
sources.title: American Indian Languages: The Historical Linguistics of Native America
sources.notes: Reference for the classification and geographic distribution of this contact language (structural adstrate node only).
